rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings like assignments
Assume input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees should use case-provided bounds, constants, or visible relations
Use boolean logic for complex conditions in AGREE expressions
generic_rules: Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in AGREE expressions
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Avoid placing assume/guarantee/eq/const in implementation annexes when describing interface behavior
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
